title: Compositions and methods of treatment
abstract: The present invention relates to a composition comprising rumen microbe derived peptides which have particular uses as antimicrobial agents.
claims:
Claims 1. A composition comprising a rumen microbe derived peptide having antimicrobial activity. 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein the peptide has a conformational structure which incorporates at least one a- helix; or is a Î²-sheet. 3. The composition as claimed in claim 2, wherein the peptide has a conformational structure which incorporates two a- helices. 4. The composition as claimed in claim 3, wherein a first a- helix comprises substantially hydrophobic residues and a second a- helix comprises substantially charged residues. 5. The composition as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the peptide is in the range of 18 to 22 amino acid residues. 6. The composition as claimed in claim 5, wherein the peptide is in the range of 19 to 20 amino acid residues. 7. The composition as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the total hydrophobicity ratio % of the peptide is in the range of 38 to 44. 8. The composition as claimed in claim 7, wherein the total hydrophobicity ratio % of 

 the peptide is in the range of 40 to 42. 9. The composition as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the peptide comprises, or consists of, the sequence of: LPRRNRW SKIWKK V VT VF S (SEQ ID No. 1); KFVRLKIYCRDKNKGRGISF (SEQ ID No. 131); SIKILKIYFIQGKRHWSF (SEQ ID No. 181); TMSLRFWRWKVR (SEQ ID NO: 135); NRFTARFRRTPWRLCLQFRQ (SEQ ID No. 3); HLRRINKLLTRIGLYRHAFG (SEQ ID No. 2); AWRWKAFRNCWRVRS S SL (SEQ ID No. 143); or variant sequence or mixtures of sequences or mimetic thereof, or C-terminal amidated peptides thereof; and optionally contains at least one D-amino acid residue; and optionally includes a deletion or substitution of 1 or more amino acids from the N or C terminus. 10. The composition of claim 9, wherein the peptide comprises a variant having at least 80% identity thereof. 11. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 10, for use in the manufacture of a medicament. 12. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 11, for use as an antimicrobial, optionally against bacteria, protozoa, viruses, and fungi. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 12, for use in the treatment of an infection. 14. The composition of claim 13, wherein the infection comprises an antibiotic resistant infection. 15. The composition of claim 14, wherein the antibiotic resistant infection comprises a Î²- lactam, macrolide, fluoroquinolone, tetracyclines, or aminoglycoside antibiotic resistant infection. 16. The composition of claim 15, wherein the infection is resistant to one or more of the following antibiotics: azithromycin, clarithromycin, dirithromycin, erythromycin, troleandomycin, roxithromycin, spiramycin, aztreonam, imipenem, meropenem, ertapenem, doripenem, panipenem/betamipron, biapenem, PZ-601, cefixime, cefdinir, cefditoren, cefoperazone, cefotaxime, cefpodoxime, ceftazidime, ceftibuten, ceftizoxime, ceftriaxone, cefepime, demeclocycline, doxycycline, minocycline, oxytetracycline, tetracycline, bacitracin, colistin, polymyxin B, ciprofloxacin, enoxacin, gatifloxacin, levofloxacin, lomefloxacin, moxifloxacin, norfloxacin, ofloxacin, and trovafloxacin. 17. The composition of any one of claims 13 to 16, wherein the infection is a bacterial or fungal infection. 18. The composition of claim 17, wherein the infection is a bacterial infection of an internal or external body surface selected from the group consisting of a surface in the oral cavity, the reproductive tract, the urinary tract, the respiratory tract, the gastrointestinal tract, the peritoneum, the middle ear, the prostate, vascular intima, the eye, including the conjunctiva or corneal tissue, lung tissue, heart valves, skin, scalp, nails, the interior of 

 wounds or the surface of adrenal, hepatic, renal, pancreatic, pituitary, thyroid, immune, ovarian, testicular, prostate, endometrial, ocular, mammary, adipose, epithelial, endothelial, neural, muscle, pulmonary, dermis, epidermis or osseous tissue; or in a body fluid selected from blood, plasma, serum, cerebrospinal fluid, GI tract contents, sputum, pulmonary secretions and semen; or in or on body tissue selected from adrenal, hepatic, renal, pancreatic, brain, heart, pituitary, thyroid, immune, ovarian, testicular, prostate, endometrial, ocular, mammary, adipose, epithelial, endothelial, neural, muscle, pulmonary, epidermis and osseous tissue. 19. The composition of either claim 17 or 18, wherein the bacterial infection is caused by a Gram negative bacterium or Gram positive bacterium. 20. The composition of any of claims 17 to 19, wherein the infection is by one or more of the following: S. aureus, E. coli, K. pneumoniae, A. baumanii, P. aeruginosa, C. jejuni, C. coli, Sal. Typhimurium, Ent. Faecalis and/or List. Monocytogenes. 21. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 10, formulated for use as a topical medicament for the treatment and/or prevention and/or management of a bacterial infection of the skin or wound of an individual. 22. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 10, for use in sterilising or sanitising a surface. 23. The composition of claim 22, wherein the surface comprises medical equipment or a wound healing dressing. 24. A wound dressing or wound dressing ointment incorporating a composition according to any one of claims 1 to 10. 25. A sterilising or sanitising formulation incorporating a composition according to any one of claims 1 to 10. 26. A method of reducing or inhibiting biofilm formation on a surface comprising applying a composition according to any one of claims 1 to 10 to said surface. 27. A method of identifying antimicrobial agents from rumen microbiota comprising the steps: (a) screening a rumen metagenomic library for potential peptides which are in the range of 18 to 22 amino acid residues having a conformational structure which either incorporates two a- helices, wherein a first a- helix comprises substantially hydrophobic residues and a second a- helix comprises substantially charged residues, or is a Î²-sheet; (b) assessing those peptides identified by screening for their antimicrobial activity and identifying those peptides which show antimicrobial activity as antimicrobial agents.
